rulings.Case Title: ERNISDELOISTINE E. FAGAN VS. MIRA WAKSMAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion For An Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, June 18, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MIRA WAKSMAN's Motion For An Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Grant motion for an order establishing that the matters specified in Requests for Admissions, Set One, be deemed admitted and true. Monetary sanctions awarded in the amount of $1,050. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to MSchreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2014, Line 15, CROSS DEFENDANT MIDLAND FUNDING LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") sufficiently pleads facts to state a cause of action under Civ. Code section 1788.15. (FACC, pars. 3, 5.) The Court did not consider the facts stated in Cross-Defendant's memorandum that are not pled in the FACC, as a demurrer can only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or facts that are judicially noticeable. (See Blank v. Kirwin (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD DEVINE VS. ROSALBA NAVARRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Wednesday, June 18, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF RICHARD DEVINE's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the mion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties should meet and confer prior to the hearing and report at the hearing on what requests and responses remain in dispute. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN PISORS VS. ANGELO FERRO et al****TRANSFERRED TO SOLANO COUNTY****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2014, Line 16, DEFENDANTs ANGELO FERRO and FERRO AUTOMOTIVE GROUP, INC.'s Motion For Change Of Venue And Request For Sanctions. Granted. (CCP § 395.5.) Venue is proper in Solano County. Defendant Ferro Automotive Group's principal place of business is 2501 Martin Road, Fairfield, California, which is located in Solano County. (Holvick Decl., Exhibit A.) The meeting minutes are not dispositive. There is no evidence that the San Francisco address was ever submitted to the Secretary of State as the principal place of business. The address of the agent for service of process is not relevant. Both sides' requests for attorneys fees and costs pursuant to CCP §396b are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: 33 COLLECTIONS LLC, et al VS. JOHN ROGERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANTs JOHN ROGERS, SPORTS CARDS PLUS INC., DIGITAL STOCK PLANET LLC DBA ARGENT IMAGES, PLANET GIANT LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted, with 10 days leave to amend. For punitive damages, Plaintiff's fraud allegations are conclusory, and not specially pled. (See Civ. Code § 3294(c).) For attorney fees, Plaintiffs have neither pled any facts nor cited the statutory bases upon which they seek attorney fees. CCP § 1021.4 is not applicable to this action; Plaintiffs admit that Defendants have not been convicted of any felony offense. (See Opp., p. 3:21-24.) Plaintiffs have not pled any facts to support an attorney fees pursuant to CCP § 1021.5. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege a prayer for punitive damages and attorney fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: 33 COLLECTIONS LLC, et al VS. JOHN ROGERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANTs JOHN ROGERS, SPORTS CARDS PLUS INC., DIGITAL STOCK PLANET LLC DBA ARGENT IMAGES, PLANET GIANT LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. None of Plaintiffs' causes of action are clearly barred by the statute of limitations on the face of the Complaint. Plaintiffs allege discovery on January 30, 2014. Whether Plaintiffs should have discovered this earlier involves a question of fact that is not the proper subject of a demurrer. For the third cause of action, Plaintiffs assert a claim for breach of the implied warranty of fitness. (Complaint, 27.) Whether the underlying contract was written, oral or implied in conduct is not germane to this cause of action. For the fifth cause of action, although injunctive relief is a remedy, "the phrase 'cause of action' is also 'commonly used in pleading as applying only to the relief sought . . . ." (McDowell v. Watson (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 1155, 1159-1160.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENT CONSTRUCTION GROUP, A GEORGIA CORPORATION et al VS. GOLDEN BEAR INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 18, 2014, Line 18, DEFENDANT GOLDEN BEAR INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Hearing required. Judge Miller discloses and will disclose at the hearing that she is a member of the same Inn of Court as plaintiff's counsel Barron Weisntein. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial, and she does not believe that a person aware of the facts might reasonably entertain a doubt that she would be able to be impartial. =(302/MJM)
